title: The Faulty Reactor
subtitle: Chapter 01 · The First Fractures
introduction: A cleanroom console waits under diagnostic light. Witness the fraction of a millimeter that must not be missed.
description: The diagnostic console is at the central power grid. A sub-millimeter offset in the grid alignment represents a massive core failure that would blackout the entire city.
observation: A physicist steadies the console, follows the quartz grid, and pauses before a thin laser line.
reconstruction: Carry the reactor calibration key, the quartz grid, and the thin diagnostic laser.
attunements: The laser is offset from its intended grid line.
The calibration key has one fresh mark.
The smallest shift would become structural loss.
revelation:
What was wrong: the diagnostic laser registered structural shift before the key was turned.

Why it mattered: the console was reading the future pressure of a reactor that had already failed, giving the physicist a fraction of a second to avert a total catastrophe.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the central quartz grid display.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Grid offset found. The diagnostic line bends before key rotation.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE LASER GRID IS DISTORTED.
capture_window.success_text: Isolated the pressure distortion before key alignment.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE REACTOR KEY SHOWS MICROSCOPIC INDENTATIONS. It is used to align core pressure levels, but the stress has already curved the diagnostic screen.
THE CENTRAL GRID DISPLAY SHOWING SHIFTING VECTORS. It curves and shifts early, registering a future pressure spike.
THE LASER WAVELENGTH IS BLUESHIFTED. A diagnostic laser line that bends under spatial pressure, registering a temporal distortion index of 1.15.

fractures.discovery_text: The laser bends around pressure that the key has not had time to release.
fractures.misstep_text: The key bears a mark, but it did not cause the fault. Follow the grid that is carrying tomorrow’s pressure.

truth_fragment.title: Early Warning
truth_fragment.summary: The console was not failing. It was giving a physicist the fraction of a second she needed to keep a city lit.
truth_fragment.revelation_text: The bent diagnostic line was not evidence that the physicist had misaligned the reactor. It was the future pressure of a failure already visible to the console, arriving early enough for her to turn the key and prevent it.
truth_fragment.archive_entry: Early Warning — a recovered intervention at the reactor console.
truth_fragment.recovered_memory_summary: A physicist reads an impossible pressure shift before a reactor catastrophe can begin.
truth_fragment.truth_statement: The grid was not predicting failure. It was giving her time to prevent it.
truth_fragment.iris_reflection: Sometimes the future speaks softly enough for someone to answer.

showcase.observation_prompt: Watch the key, then the quartz grid. The line bends before the hand can make a mistake.
showcase.fracture_prompt: The key is not to blame. Find the pressure that reached the console before the reactor could create it.
showcase.synchronization_prompt: Hold the warning with the Iris. Give the future enough time to become preventable.
false_leads: The calibration key is marked, but it is a response to the warning, not the cause of it.
The console looks faulty, yet the bend begins inside pressure that has not arrived.
The laser is only the messenger. Follow the grid that knows about the failure too soon.
